committee.name: Accounts Payable Manager
interests: How the Review Queue surfaces flagged reports so I can triage exception backlog without opening every record
Whether blocking exceptions — missing receipts, FX gaps, inactive-category flags — actually stop an approval or just warn
How GL coding works per line and whether I can assign default GL accounts per expense category to cut repetitive work
The separation-of-duties enforcement: batch preparer versus batch poster, and whether the system hard-blocks a self-pay
How the Exception Queue distinguishes duplicate-receipt flags from policy-violation flags so I route each correctly
The NACHA ACH export format and whether it maps to our bank's file spec without manual reformatting
How the SLA dashboard breaks cycle time by handoff so I can see if delays sit with managers or in my own queue
Whether the Spend Analytics materialized views can be refreshed on demand before month-end close or only on a fixed schedule
How a closed accounting period blocks line postings — and whether I can reopen a period for a correction without losing the close
How adjustments to an approved line are audited, specifically whether the adjustment reason and the re-approval are both in the hash-chained trail
The executive co-approval bar on my dashboard — which reports are held there and how I clear the hold without chasing the controller offline
How the CSV export from All Reports respects my current filter so I get the exact population I need for a reconciliation
Whether the Controls and Compliance panel can show me payment batches posted in a given period alongside their CSV/NACHA exports
How taxable-reimbursement flags surface — meals, gifts, non-deductible entertainment — so I can pass the right amounts to payroll
Whether the audit log CSV includes the hash column so I can run an integrity check offline without depending on the in-app panel
decisionCriteria: Every line on every approved report must carry a GL account and a cost center before I can post — if the system lets an approval through without that, it fails my review
Blocking exceptions must truly block: a missing receipt above the $25 threshold should prevent submission, not just flag it after the fact
Separation of duties has to be absolute — the same user who builds a payment batch cannot post it; if I can work around that in the UI, it's a control failure
The SLA dashboard must show aging by handoff stage, not just total cycle time, so I know whether my team is the bottleneck or managers are
The NACHA export must be ready for direct bank upload without me editing the file — any field-mapping error that requires manual fixes is a deal-breaker
Period close must prevent new postings to closed periods and give me a reopen path for corrections that creates an auditable record
Spend analytics must be filterable by department and cost center, not just company-wide, because each department manager wants their own number
Fraud monitor and duplicate-receipt detection need to fire before I've already approved — not surface after the fact on a compliance report
The audit trail must be immutable and hash-chained — I need to show an external auditor a chain they can verify independently
Advance handling: when an employee received a travel advance, I need the net reimbursable — not gross — to flow through to the payment batch
I need to see which reports are in my queue versus already processed so the dashboard KPIs reflect real current workload, not stale counts
Any adjustment I make to an approved line must reopen the report to accounting review, not silently change the amount that already flowed to the batch
The exception queue must let me waive a flag with a documented reason — a waiver without a reason trail is an audit finding waiting to happen
The Controls and Compliance export must include every field an auditor would need: report number, employee, amount, GL, approver, dates, and hash
goals: Close every accounting period on time with zero GL-unassigned lines sitting in approved reports
Get payment batch creation down to under two hours at month-end by having the review queue clear of blocking exceptions before I start
Eliminate the situation where an employee gets reimbursed twice — blocked by the system, not caught by a manual check
Maintain a clean separation-of-duties posture that passes an external audit without me assembling evidence manually
Reduce the exception backlog visible on my dashboard to zero before every period close, not just resolve it in bulk the day before
Have the SLA breach table show me exactly which reports are past 48 hours in accounting review so I can act before the controller escalates
Export the payment batch as a NACHA file and upload it directly to the bank without intermediate spreadsheet manipulation
Give each department manager their own spend-by-category view from Spend Analytics so they stop emailing me for monthly reports
Track which reports needed a line-amount adjustment and why, so I can identify employees who habitually miscategorize expenses
Have the fraud monitor surface duplicate merchants or unusual patterns before they reach the reimbursement queue
Document every waived exception with a reason so the audit log tells the full story when we're reviewed
Stop chasing managers by email when their approvals age past 48 hours — see it on the SLA dashboard and escalate from there
Ensure taxable reimbursements are flagged before period close so payroll gets the right imputed-income numbers without a reconciliation scramble
objections: Our current process has the GL coding happening in QuickBooks — if expense.vin holds a QuickBooks mapping code per GL account but does not sync, I'm doing double entry every period
The exception queue in seed mode shows per-violation detail, but in live mode it's a per-report aggregate — I need the per-violation breakdown to triage efficiently, not just a count
If manager delegation is stored but approval routing does not yet consult it, then a manager's absence still stalls my queue even though the system knows about the delegation
The fraud monitor and taxable-reimbursement panels say they need line-level data not available in live mode — so I lose the controls I most need when I go to production
ACH payment requires a poster who differs from the preparer, but if we only have one finance user, that workflow is broken by design in our office
Reopening a closed period for a correction is admin-only — if I need a correction at 8 pm before month-end and the admin isn't available, I'm blocked
The All Reports CSV exports only the filtered view — if someone clears filters before exporting, we may lose track of what population was actually sent to the auditor
Receipt capture is file upload only — employees uploading photos of crumpled paper receipts is still manual work for me to verify quality
The Spend Analytics refresh is manual in live mode — if I forget to click Refresh before I build the board report, I'm presenting stale numbers
No receipt OCR means I have to eyeball each receipt against the entered amount; a high-volume month still ties up my team manually
If the approval matrix bands are governance-driven and not editable in the UI, I can't adjust the $2,500 executive co-approval threshold without a backend change
I'm not sure the audit log CSV is sufficient for our external auditor — they may want a direct database query, and the CSV being the only export path concerns me
openQuestions: When I open a report in the Review Queue and assign GL accounts to every line, does the system prevent me from clicking Approve and Code until all lines are coded — or does it let me submit and catch it server-side?
If I waive a blocking exception — say a missing receipt for a $30 meal — where exactly does the waiver reason appear in the audit log, and is it exported in the CSV?
The SLA dashboard shows a 48-hour target for accounting review — does the clock start when the report enters my queue, or when the manager approved it?
When I build a payment batch and a second finance user needs to post it, does the system send the poster a notification, or do I have to tell them out of band?
Can I filter the Reimbursement Queue by payment method — ACH versus check versus payroll — before I build the batch, so I'm not mixing methods in one export?
If a report has already been approved and I need to adjust a line amount, what exactly does re-entering accounting review mean — does the manager see it again, or just accounting?
The executive co-approval bar shows over-threshold reports awaiting a controller — if the controller is out, who can I escalate to and how does that show in the audit trail?
Does the Exception Queue let me resolve a duplicate-receipt flag differently than a policy-violation flag, or is the only option resolve or waive for both types?
When I export NACHA, does the file include the payment reference I entered, or does the system auto-generate one — and can I set a batch reference that appears on the bank statement?
The Controls and Compliance screen shows a fraud monitor panel — what specific signals trigger a fraud flag, and at what point in the workflow does it fire?
If an employee submits a report in EUR and I need to verify the exchange rate used, where do I see the FX rate that was applied and when it was last updated?
Can I run the SLA check from the Accounting Dashboard and see the breaches list in one click, or do I have to navigate to the SLA dashboard separately each time?
When I close an accounting period, does the system immediately block all new postings to that period, or is there a grace window — and does the block apply to admin overrides too?
If a report is marked reimbursed but the employee says they never received payment, what information does the report detail show me — reference, method, date — to help me investigate?
The All Reports screen has a CSV export for auditors — does the export include every status, or only processed reports, and can an auditor export directly or do they need me to run it?
How does the system handle a report where the employee entered a mileage trip and a regular expense line in the same report — do both feed the same GL coding step, and does mileage have its own GL?
Are open exceptions visible to the employee or only to accounting — because if employees can see their own exception flags before submission, it might cut my backlog significantly?

committee.name: Operations Manager
interests: How quickly my team's submitted reports clear manager review and reach accounting, so staff aren't waiting weeks for reimbursement
Whether the manager approval flow requires me to log into a separate portal or works from a notification and one-click action
How the Department Activity screen shows me each team member's spend so I can spot outliers without pulling a finance report myself
Whether I can see all reports my team has in flight — under review, awaiting payment, returned for clarification — from a single view
How the delegation feature works when I'm traveling — can I hand my approval queue to my deputy without it creating an audit gap
Whether the clarification workflow gives the employee clear instructions on what to fix, or just pings them back with a generic return notice
How the approval matrix bands work — which of my team's reports will get held for executive co-approval and slow down reimbursement
Whether I can approve a report on my phone when I'm offsite, since I'm rarely at a desk for the full workday
How the system handles an employee who submitted under the wrong cost center — can I reroute it without rejecting and making them start over
Whether the SLA dashboard lets me see if my approvals are the bottleneck, not just whether accounting is slow
How the mileage rate gets applied — I want staff to trust the calculated amount rather than manually computing miles times rate
Whether the reimbursement tracking screen gives my staff enough visibility that they stop asking me when their money is coming
decisionCriteria: My team's average reimbursement cycle should drop below two weeks — if the system doesn't shorten the manager-to-payment timeline, the pain of switching isn't worth it
Approving a report should take under three minutes from notification to done — if I have to navigate more than two screens, my team won't get timely approvals
The clarification reason I write when I return a report must be visible to the employee on their Clarifications screen so they fix the right thing the first time
Delegation must be fully auditable — I need to know which approvals my deputy acted on during my absence without calling them
The Department Activity screen should show me both spend and report count per team member so I can identify who is over-submitting, not just over-spending
If a staff member submits with the wrong cost center, there should be a correction path that doesn't reject the whole report and lose the original receipt uploads
Staff should be able to track their own reimbursement status without asking me — if I still field those questions, the employee-side UX has failed
The approval matrix threshold for executive co-approval should not surprise me — I need to know before approving a report whether it will be held at the controller level
The system should not let me accidentally approve my own expense if I also have an employee role — separation of duties has to hold at my login
Mobile usability matters: if I can only approve on a desktop, reports will age past 48 hours every time I travel
goals: Get my team's reimbursements paid within ten business days of submission, down from our current three-to-four-week average
Handle my approval queue in under thirty minutes per week by having exception-free reports presented to me cleanly
Never have a report sit in my queue past 48 hours without a reminder surfacing it on my dashboard
Understand my department's monthly expense patterns from the Department Activity screen without waiting for a finance report
Eliminate the back-and-forth where I reject a report for a missing receipt only for the employee to resubmit with the same error
Use the delegation feature confidently during travel so the approval queue never stalls while I'm out
Have staff trust the mileage calculation enough that they stop bringing personal spreadsheets to verify the rate
Reduce the number of times I field questions about reimbursement status by pointing staff to their own Reimbursement Tracking screen
Know which of my team members are consistently over the per-diem thresholds so I can coach before the expense is already submitted
Have a clean audit record of every approval I've made so that if a report is questioned later, I can show exactly what I saw and when I approved it
objections: If the delegation feature stores the assignment but approval routing doesn't yet consult it in live mode, then handing off my queue to a deputy doesn't actually work — my team's approvals stall while I'm traveling

The manager dashboard shows reports awaiting my approval, but I can't approve from the dashboard — I have to open each report individually; for a team of fifteen, that's fifteen separate page loads every day

If an employee picks the wrong cost center and I can't correct it as a manager — only accounting can adjust an approved line — then I'm sending reports back unnecessarily instead of fixing a one-field error

I don't always know which of my team's reports will trip the $2,500 executive co-approval threshold until after I've approved; I'd rather see a flag before I act so I can manage the employee's expectation

The Department Activity screen only counts spend at accounting review and beyond — a team member's report in manager review shows a report count but $0 in spend, which makes the chart misleading mid-period
There's no mention of email or push notifications when a report enters my queue — if I have to remember to check the dashboard, approvals will age
My deputies aren't all formal 'managers' in the system — if delegation only routes to other manager-role accounts, I can't delegate to my senior coordinator who actually covers for me
I'm concerned that rejecting a report — rather than requesting clarification — loses all the receipt attachments and forces the employee to re-upload everything
The 48-hour SLA target assumes I'm reviewing daily — during a busy field week that's not realistic, and I don't want an SLA breach against my name for a normal travel week
If expense approvals pile onto my plate, can I bulk-approve routine in-policy claims and only stop on the exceptions?
openQuestions: When a report lands in my manager queue, do I get an email or a notification — and if not, how am I expected to know it's waiting?
If I request clarification on a report, does the employee see my exact note on their Clarifications screen, or just a generic 'returned' status?
Can I see the total amount of a report before I open it, so I know whether it will trigger the executive co-approval hold before I approve?
After I approve a report, how long does it typically sit in accounting review before the employee is paid — can I see that average somewhere?
The delegation screen says routing doesn't yet consult the delegation in live mode — when will that be fixed, and what should I tell my deputy in the meantime?
Does the Department Activity screen update in real time, or do I need to refresh it to see this morning's approved reports?
If I accidentally approve a report that should have been returned, can I pull it back from accounting review or is it too late once I've approved?
What happens to a report in my queue if the employee withdraws it — does it disappear from my dashboard immediately or sit there flagged?
Can my team members see who their approver is before they submit, so they know to contact me if something is time-sensitive?
If a team member submits in CAD because they were on a Canadian trip, does the approval flow show me the USD equivalent, or do I have to do the conversion mentally?
Is there any view that shows me all of my team's reports across all statuses — not just the ones waiting for my approval — so I can answer a staff question without asking accounting?
When I'm on the report detail and I click Approve, does the system tell me right then that it will be held for executive co-approval, or does that happen silently after I submit?
The clarification workflow sends the report back to the employee — how many times can a report go back and forth before it's escalated or flagged as problematic?
If two of my team members submitted reports on the same day for the same conference, will the duplicate-receipt detection fire even though they're separate people?
Can I see the audit history of a specific report from the manager detail view, or do I have to ask accounting to look it up in the audit log?

committee.name: Department Manager
interests: Whether the approval workflow will actually move fast enough that my staff aren't fronting expenses for more than two pay cycles
How the Department Activity screen breaks down spend by team member so I can see if one person is driving most of the expense volume
Whether the mileage rate applied in the report wizard is the current IRS rate or one I have to update manually each January
How the clarification process works — whether I can give staff a specific note about what to fix rather than a generic return
Whether my team can submit from mobile — most of my staff are in the field and don't have regular desk access
How the advance received field works when a staff member was given petty cash before a trip — does it offset the reimbursement automatically
Whether the system flags per-diem overages before submission or only after accounting reviews them
How cost centers map to my department — whether my budget lines are visible in reports from my team or buried in accounting's view
Whether rejected reports lose all receipt attachments, forcing staff to rescan everything
How the period-from and period-to fields on a report relate to the accounting period — whether a late submission can go into the right month or is stuck in the current open period
Whether I can see reports my staff submitted that are not yet assigned to me for approval — currently I find out about problems only when accounting flags something
decisionCriteria: My staff should receive reimbursement within ten business days of submitting a clean report — if approval handoffs routinely add weeks, the tool doesn't solve my team's problem
The clarification note I write must reach the employee with enough specificity to fix the issue on resubmission — a vague return is worse than no return
Mileage calculations must use the current IRS rate automatically, not a rate I have to remember to update; a wrong rate means I'm approving incorrect amounts
Staff should be able to submit a receipt photo from their phone without needing a desktop — if mobile upload isn't reliable, field staff will still hand me paper
I need to see my team's total approved spend for the month before month-end so I can compare it to my budget without waiting for a finance report
Rejected reports must not destroy receipt attachments — if my staff have to rescan and resubmit from scratch after a rejection, they will avoid the system
The approval matrix should not surprise me with an executive co-approval hold for a routine $3,000 conference registration — I need to understand the thresholds before my staff submit
If a staff member submitted to the wrong cost center, there should be a correction path that doesn't require a full rejection and resubmission
Policy warning messages at submission time must be specific enough that staff understand what they violated — 'policy issue' with no detail is useless on the field
Whether I can see my department’s pending and reimbursed expenses against budget without asking finance for a report.
goals: Have every clean report reimbursed within ten business days so staff stop fronting out-of-pocket expenses for extended periods
Spend less than twenty minutes per week on my approval queue by seeing clear, exception-free reports with the full context I need to act
Give every returning report a specific clarification note so the employee fixes it on the first resubmit, not the third
Know my department's month-to-date approved spend from the Department Activity screen without asking accounting for a report
Prevent my staff from submitting meals over the $75 per-diem threshold by having the warn flag visible before they hit submit
Have mileage reimbursements computed automatically at the current IRS rate so I'm not manually checking each trip against a posted rate
Stop fielding questions about reimbursement status by giving staff direct access to their Reimbursement Tracking screen
Avoid the 30-minute scramble at period close by having my team's reports submitted and approved before the last week of the month
Understand which expense categories are consuming the most of my budget so I can coach before a threshold is hit, not after it's already been approved
Stop being the bottleneck on my team’s reimbursements while still catching the claims that break policy.
objections: My staff will not consistently upload receipts from their phones if the file-upload process is cumbersome — I need to see the mobile experience before I commit
The $2,500 executive co-approval threshold will regularly catch our conference travel and equipment purchases, adding days to reimbursement for routine expenses
Per-diem warn policies only flag after submission — if staff don't see the warning until accounting review, they've already spent the money and expect full reimbursement
I don't receive any notification when a report lands in my queue — I have to remember to check the manager dashboard, which means approvals will age during busy weeks
If I'm the approver and also have expenses myself, I want to confirm the system blocks me from approving my own report — I don't want to create a controls finding by accident

The Department Activity screen shows spend only from accounting review onward — during an active travel week, most reports will still show $0 because they're in manager review, making the chart useless mid-period
I'm not confident that rejecting a report is reversible — if I reject by mistake rather than requesting clarification, I may have killed the report entirely
Period dates on the report are entered by the employee, but the actual accounting period is controlled by admin — if those don't align, I'm not sure whose period wins for month-end posting
Will I be expected to police receipts line-by-line, or does expense.vin flag the policy breaks for me?
If someone submits a duplicate or out-of-policy claim, does it reach me or slip straight through to payment?
openQuestions: When I click Request Clarification on a report, does my note appear word-for-word on the employee's Clarifications screen, or is it reformatted or truncated?
Is there a way to see all my team's in-flight reports — not just the ones in my approval queue — so I can answer a staff question about a report that accounting already has?
The mileage step calculates amount as miles times the current IRS rate — where does that rate come from and who updates it when the IRS changes it mid-year?
What happens to all the receipt attachments if I reject a report instead of requesting clarification — does the employee have to re-upload them on the new submission?
Does the policy warn for a $76 meal show up to the employee before they submit, or does it only appear in the exceptions panel when accounting reviews it?
Can I see a summary of all reports I've approved this month — not just what's currently pending — so I have a record if a report is questioned later?
If a staff member submits a report while I'm on PTO and delegation isn't live yet, who handles the approval — does it just sit in my queue?
The advance received field subtracts from the net reimbursable — if the employee entered the wrong advance amount, can I correct it as the approving manager or does it go back to them?
Does the system tell the employee which specific policy was violated when their report is returned — or just that there's a policy issue?
When a report is held for executive co-approval after I approve it, does the employee see a status that explains the hold — or do they just see it hasn't moved?
Can I approve a report from my phone's browser, or does the report detail not render usably on mobile?
If I request clarification on a report and the employee resubmits, does it come back to me first or go straight to accounting?
How does the cost-center field on an expense line relate to my department budget — can I see a budget-vs-actual comparison anywhere in the system?
Is there a way for me to see my team's average reimbursement cycle time — from submission to payment — so I can tell staff what to expect when they ask?
If a staff member submits with the wrong department or wrong manager assigned, how is that corrected without losing the original receipts and starting over?

committee.name: Administration Manager
interests: How the Configuration screen's six tabs let me manage the full system without IT involvement — hub users, categories, policies, GL accounts, cost centers, FX rates, and accounting periods
Whether adding a new expense category with a default GL account and receipt threshold requires a backend change or is entirely self-service from the Categories tab
How the accounting period state machine works — lock versus close versus reopen — and whether I can reopen a period for a correction without losing the original close timestamp in the audit log
Whether GL account and cost center changes I make in Configuration take effect immediately on new reports or require a period boundary
How the policy enforcement settings — warn versus block — can be tuned per policy code without adding new policy keys, which requires a backend change
How the Governance tab's approval-matrix bands are managed — whether I can adjust the $500, $2,500 thresholds myself or only read them in the UI
Whether the hub-user create and edit flows persist to the live backend or remain local-only, and what the workaround is until a live route ships
How the FX rate editor works when we need to add a currency our team just started using on a new client project in Canada
Whether the audit log's hash-chained integrity is verifiable by me as admin, or only by accounting running the Controls and Compliance export
How the Platform Overview dashboard's integrity panel distinguishes a real chain break from a seed-mode artifact so I'm not chasing false alarms
Whether the admin override — force a status with a reason — is available in live mode or only in seed mode, because I need it for edge cases at period close
How delegation records I can see in the Delegations tab map to actual approval routing, especially now that live routing doesn't yet consult them
Whether the mileage rate and receipt-rule editors are self-service or read-only in the UI pending a live writer
How the Reset Demo button is scoped — is there any risk of it appearing or being accessible in a production context rather than only in seed mode
decisionCriteria: I must be able to add or archive expense categories, GL accounts, and cost centers myself without filing a support ticket or waiting for a developer
Accounting period management — lock, close, reopen — must be fully in my hands with each action audited so I can defend the state of the books at any point in time
Policy enforcement changes (warn versus block, threshold tweaks per policy code) must be editable from the Policies tab, not require a backend deployment
If hub-user create and edit don't persist to the live backend yet, there must be a documented workaround that doesn't leave ghost users in the system
The FX rate table must let me add a new currency pair without developer involvement — we pick up new international work unpredictably and can't wait for a backend change
Admin override to force a status with a reason must be available in live mode for edge-case corrections, not only in seed mode
The Controls and Compliance panel must let me export the full audit chain as CSV so I can hand it to an external auditor without giving them admin access
The mileage rate and receipt rule editors must either be fully functional or show a clear 'pending' state so I know not to rely on them for live policy changes
The approval-matrix bands (the $500 and $2,500 thresholds) must be configurable by me, not hard-coded — our reimbursement patterns don't fit a one-size default
Any configuration change I make must create an audit log entry so I can answer the question 'who changed that and when' without guessing
I need to be able to preview any role — employee, manager, accounting — from the admin account so I can reproduce a reported issue without creating a test account
The Delegations tab must show me all active delegations and let me revoke any of them, not just the ones I personally created, so I can clean up stale delegations when a manager leaves
goals: Be the single point of control for system configuration — categories, GL, policies, cost centers, FX, and periods — without waiting for IT
Close each accounting period cleanly and on schedule, with a full audit trail of every lock, close, and reopen action
Keep the expense category list and GL account list current as the business adds service lines and cost centers without filing backend change requests
Have the platform integrity panel on my dashboard show me a clean chain every morning so I can start the day confident the audit trail is intact
Reduce onboarding time for new employees by managing hub-user setup entirely from the Hub Users tab once live user writes ship
Give department managers self-service access to their cost-center spend without granting them admin access to Configuration
Maintain a clean separation-of-duties record by auditing every delegation, override, and period change from the audit log export
Resolve edge-case report situations — wrong status, stuck approval — using the admin override rather than asking engineering to patch the database
Have FX rates current before any international report reaches accounting review, so FX-missing exceptions don't pile up at period end
Know immediately when an open exception count on the dashboard rises above zero so I can alert the accounting team before the backlog grows
objections: Hub-user create and edit are listed as local-only in live mode — that means my user management is not real until a live route ships, which is a significant gap for day-one operations
The approval-matrix editor is read-only in the UI — if I can't adjust the $500 and $2,500 bands myself, I'm dependent on a developer change every time our approval policy evolves
Mileage rate and receipt-rule editors are marked as not yet having a live writer — so the IRS rate change I need to apply in January requires a backend deployment rather than a Configuration update
Admin override to force a status is only in seed mode on live — without it, a report stuck in an incorrect state at month-end becomes a manual database intervention
The Reset Demo button exists in Configuration — even if it only fires in seed mode, having it visible in a production UI is a risk I need to understand before going live
Reopening a closed period is admin-only, which is correct, but if I'm unavailable at period close and no other admin exists, the organization is blocked on corrections
Policy code is read-only on the live Policies tab — I can change enforcement mode and archive, but I cannot add a net-new policy type without a backend change
The Controls and Compliance fraud monitor says it needs line-level data not available in live mode — one of the controls I'm responsible for demonstrating to auditors doesn't compute in production
If delegation routing isn't live yet, delegations I create in the Delegations tab give managers a false sense of coverage — I need to communicate the limitation clearly to every manager
There's no bulk import for FX rates — if we onboard a new project with expenses in three new currencies, I have to add three rate records manually one at a time
openQuestions: When I archive a category in the Categories tab, what happens to existing reports that have lines categorized under it — do those lines flag as inactive-category exceptions retroactively?
The hub-user create and edit writes are local-only in live mode — is the manager-assignment field the only live write, and if so, how do I set initial roles for new staff before that ships?
Can I reopen a closed accounting period and re-close it after a correction, and does each reopen and re-close create separate audit log entries so the history is complete?
The Governance tab shows the approval-matrix bands as read-only — is there a roadmap item to make those editable in the UI, or will they always require a backend change?
When I add a new FX rate for a currency we haven't used before, does it take effect immediately on reports already in draft, or only on new reports created after the save?
The admin overview dashboard shows Open Exceptions as a KPI — does that count include exceptions in all statuses (open, waived) or only open unresolved ones?
If I use the admin override to force a report to a different status, does the reason I enter appear in the audit log alongside my admin account, or is it attributed to the original submitter?
Can I see the full report detail of any employee's report from my admin account — including receipts and line detail — without being in the approval workflow for that report?
The mileage rate display says 'current IRS rate' — where is that rate stored, who updates it, and what happens between January 1 and whenever the update is applied?
Is there a way to bulk-archive expense categories at year-end when we restructure the chart of accounts, or does each archival require an individual save?
When the Controls and Compliance screen shows the separation-of-duties summary, does it actively flag any reports where the approver and submitter were the same person, or is it an aggregate rollup?
If I close a period and a report is still in manager review with a date in that period, does the system block the approval or block the line posting — and who gets the error?
Does the Delegations tab show me delegations created by all managers or only ones I set up personally — and can I revoke a delegation a manager created without their involvement?
The Platform Overview integrity panel says 'server-enforced in live mode' — what exactly does that mean for my monitoring, and when would I see an integrity failure I should act on?
Is there an export from the Configuration tab — categories, policies, GL accounts, cost centers — that I can use for a year-end configuration review without screenshotting each tab?
Can I run the Spend Analytics refresh from the Configuration screen, or does that only live on the Analytics screen accessible to accounting?

committee.name: Payroll Manager
interests: Whether taxable reimbursements — personal use, non-deductible meals, certain gifts — are flagged in expense.vin before the payment batch is built so I have the imputed-income figures before payroll runs

How the payment method field on a queued report distinguishes ACH from Payroll from Check, and whether reports routed to Payroll method can be identified in a batch export I can pull into our payroll system
Whether the net reimbursable amount — after advance deduction — is clearly separated from gross approved spend in the reports accounting hands me, because payroll uses the net figure
How mileage reimbursements are flagged or coded differently from general expense lines so I can confirm they're non-taxable at the IRS rate and not accidentally included in taxable income

Whether the export from the Reimbursement Queue or All Reports gives me the per-report payment method, amount, and employee name in a format I can reconcile against a payroll register without reformatting
How the FX normalization works on reports submitted in foreign currencies — whether payroll always sees a USD net or whether I need to apply my own rate
Whether the Controls and Compliance tax panel identifies which specific reports or line items were flagged as taxable so I can add them to imputed income by employee

How the separation-of-duties controls around marking a report reimbursed interact with payroll-method reports — if a payroll report is marked reimbursed in expense.vin, who reconciles that against the payroll run
Whether there is any double-payment risk between expense.vin's reimbursement tracking and a separate payroll batch — the system says duplicate payment is blocked, but only within expense.vin
How the payment reference field on a reimbursed report is populated, and whether I can use it to cross-reference expense.vin records against our payroll or bank statements
decisionCriteria: Taxable-reimbursement flags must be available to me before each payroll run, not discovered at year-end — if I can't pull a taxable-items list by employee per period, I'm doing a year-end scramble
The Payroll payment method must be identifiable in the CSV export so I know which reimbursements are flowing through my payroll run versus direct ACH without manually sorting the file
Net reimbursable — advance-adjusted — must be the figure in the export, not gross approved; running payroll on a gross figure overpays employees who received advances
Mileage reimbursements at the IRS rate must be clearly coded or labeled so I can exclude them from taxable income with confidence, not assumption
The system must block double reimbursement within expense.vin, and I need to understand how to reconcile payroll-method reports to prevent paying the same expense through both systems
The payment reference field should be reliably populated — either by auto-generate or by the accounting user — so I can use it as the cross-reference key in my payroll reconciliation
The Controls and Compliance export must be detailed enough to isolate taxable events by employee and period, not just show a total policy-violation count
If expense.vin marks a payroll-method report as 'reimbursed' before I've processed payroll, there must be a clear workflow so accounting doesn't mark paid on something I haven't run yet
Whether reimbursements that run through payroll are clearly separated from taxable pay so I don’t misreport them.
Whether the reimbursement export reconciles cleanly to what actually lands in each employee’s pay run.
goals: Have the taxable reimbursement list from the Controls and Compliance screen ready two business days before each payroll close so I can include imputed income without a last-minute scramble
Pull one filtered CSV from All Reports or the Reimbursement Queue that gives me every Payroll-method report for the period, with employee, net reimbursable, and payment reference, ready to import
Eliminate the end-of-year W-2 correction scramble by having taxable-meal and non-deductible-entertainment flags identified and attributed to employees at the time of reimbursement
Confirm that mileage reimbursements are calculated at the current IRS rate so I have a defensible non-taxable basis without separately verifying each trip
Reconcile every expense.vin reimbursement against the payroll run or bank statement using the payment reference as the key, reducing open items in the reconciliation to zero
Understand exactly which reports are routed to Payroll method versus ACH so I'm not surprised by an employee asking why their expense reimbursement appeared as a payroll line
Have a clear handoff protocol with accounting so expense.vin reports on Payroll method aren't marked reimbursed until I've confirmed inclusion in the payroll run
Reduce manual lookups by having the net-reimbursable amount visible in the export rather than computing advance offsets from a separate field
Pay approved reimbursements in the right pay cycle without manual re-keying from expense.vin.
Keep a clean separation between expense reimbursements and wages for accurate year-end reporting.
objections: The Controls and Compliance taxable-reimbursement panel says it needs line-level data not available in live mode — the one panel I depend on most for payroll compliance doesn't function in production
The payment method on a queued report — ACH, Payroll, Check, Cash, Other — is set by accounting, not by payroll; I have no way to flag a report as needing payroll routing before it reaches the queue

If accounting marks a Payroll-method report as reimbursed in expense.vin before I run payroll, the employee shows as paid in expense.vin but hasn't actually received anything yet, creating a reconciliation nightmare

The duplicate-payment block only prevents double payment within expense.vin — if the same expense is submitted in expense.vin and also handed to payroll through our existing process, there's no cross-system check
There's no mention of a year-end taxable-reimbursement summary by employee — if I have to reconstruct it from the audit log export one report at a time, that's weeks of work

The advance received field offsets the net reimbursable, but if I'm looking at the export and both gross and net are present, I need to confirm which column to use for payroll — a misread means overpayment
Mileage reimbursements use the displayed IRS rate, but if that rate wasn't updated at the IRS rate change date, employees may have been over- or under-reimbursed without either of us knowing

I don't have an accounting or admin role — I'll be working from reports and exports shared by accounting; if the export doesn't include the fields I need by default, every period becomes a custom request
There's no integration between expense.vin's reimbursement records and our payroll system — I'm reconciling two separate exports manually, which is exactly the process I was hoping to eliminate
If an expense is approved after the pay cut-off, does it cleanly roll to the next cycle or fall through the cracks?
openQuestions: Does the Controls and Compliance tax panel work in live mode, or does the 'needs line-level data' limitation mean I cannot see taxable reimbursements in production — and if so, what is the workaround?

When accounting queues a report for reimbursement and sets the method to Payroll, does the system distinguish that report in any export column so I can filter to Payroll-method items without manually sorting?

The net reimbursable is the approved total minus the advance received — in the CSV export from All Reports or the Reimbursement Queue, which column reflects that net figure rather than gross approved?
Are mileage reimbursements shown as a separate line type in the report detail and in any export, or do they appear as regular expense lines categorized under a mileage category?
Is there a way to pull a list of all taxable-flagged line items by employee for a given accounting period — the kind of report I need to prepare the W-2 imputed-income entries?
When a report marked as Payroll method is set to 'reimbursed' in expense.vin, is there any date stamp or confirmation field I can use to verify it was included in a specific payroll run?
Does the payment reference field auto-populate from a batch reference, or does accounting enter it manually — and if manual, is it required before the reimbursed status can be set?
If an employee received a $500 advance and submitted a $700 report, does the export show $700 approved and $200 net reimbursable as separate fields, or only the $200 net?
Can the All Reports CSV be filtered to a single accounting period and a single payment method before export, or do I have to download everything and filter it in a spreadsheet?
The expense.vin approval matrix has an executive co-approval threshold — does that affect how the report appears in exports or only in the approval workflow?

Is there any built-in reconciliation view that shows me expense.vin reimbursements alongside what was actually paid, or is matching expense.vin records to payroll or bank records entirely a manual process on my end?
If the IRS mileage rate is updated mid-year, can accounting update it in Configuration and have it apply only to new mileage entries — or does it recalculate existing approved trips?

For entertainment expenses flagged as policy violations — over $250 — does the violation flag also indicate whether the expense is fully non-deductible or only partially, or is that a determination I still have to make myself?

Does the audit log export include enough detail — employee, category, amount, date, flag type — for me to reconstruct a per-employee taxable-reimbursement schedule for a prior period if we're audited?
Is there a way to set up a recurring export or report that fires before each payroll close, or do I have to ask accounting to run the All Reports export and share it with me every period?
